jokes.text: At a hospital, the treatment is to lie in a bed. Whatever's wrong with a human being, lie down. Doctor never says, 'Well, your condition isn't as bad as we thought. We want you to kind of lean against a doorjamb for seven to 10 days.'
jokes.setup: Jerry observes the universal hospital treatment
jokes.punchline: The absurd alternative of leaning against a doorjamb
jokes.explanation: Classic Seinfeld observational humor with an absurd visual. Well-structured setup/punchline with good economy.

jokes.text: Unless you go to the emergency room... then it's a chair. You've been shot, stabbed, run over: 'Have a seat.'
jokes.setup: Emergency rooms are for serious injuries
jokes.punchline: Casual 'have a seat' response to severe trauma
jokes.explanation: Great economy and ironic contrast between severity of injuries and casual response.

jokes.text: It's like a local news team in there. The doctor's the anchorman. He's got white hair, he's in charge. The nurse is the feature reporter, you know. You listen to her, but it's always, 'And now, back to your doctor.'
jokes.setup: Hospital staff as news team analogy
jokes.punchline: Nurse as feature reporter who gets cut off
jokes.explanation: Creative extended metaphor with perfect execution. The 'back to your doctor' line is brilliantly observed.

jokes.text: The orderlies come in with food and bedpans. That's like your sports and weather.
jokes.punchline: Orderlies as sports and weather - the mundane essentials
jokes.explanation: Solid continuation of the metaphor with excellent economy, though less impactful than the setup.

jokes.text: Then the baby's head comes out, and I'm screaming. My brother, who's videotaping, turns green. His eyes roll up in his head, and he blacks out. He drops the camera, the camera breaks.
jokes.punchline: Chain reaction of disasters during delivery
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and cringe factor, typical of Elaine's oversharing tendencies.

jokes.text: -And then a doctor says... -Hey, thanks. That's enough.
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing for Jerry's character to shut down TMI. Good economy.

jokes.text: Suck, suck, suck. Yeah, yeah, yeah. -Look at that, Jerry, look at that. -Yeah, I looked. I saw it.
jokes.explanation: Captures the awkwardness well through Jerry's response, though setup is obvious.

jokes.text: My friend's wife's breasts are out. Why would I be uncomfortable?
jokes.setup: Jerry claims not to be uncomfortable
jokes.punchline: The obvious reason he should be uncomfortable
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic delivery typical of Jerry, highlighting social awkwardness.

jokes.text: -So how long do they do this? -Year or two. No break? -After that comes the weaning. -After sucking comes weaning? -First sucking, then weaning. -You gotta wean. -Gotta wean. -Must wean.
jokes.explanation: Classic Seinfeld escalation where they become obsessed with repeating a word. Very quotable and character-driven.

jokes.text: You open the door to the car... you walk right into the hospital. You can't beat that spot.
jokes.punchline: His obsessive pride in parking proximity
jokes.explanation: Perfect George behavior - obsessing over minor victories. High character integration.

jokes.text: Maybe the baby would like to see the spot. A positive, uplifting message to start his life out with, huh? You can still get a great spot in this city if you apply yourself.
jokes.setup: George wants to show off his parking spot
jokes.punchline: Treating parking as life wisdom for a newborn
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing George's warped priorities. Absurd but perfectly in character.

jokes.text: -Did you give him the room number? -Yeah, 1397. 1937.
jokes.setup: Asking about giving Kramer the room number
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection joke with good economy but predictable setup.

jokes.text: 1937, 1937. Excuse me. Do you know where the elevator is? Yeah, it's right around the corner there. 1937.
jokes.setup: Kramer looking for wrong room number
jokes.punchline: Repeating the wrong number to everyone
jokes.explanation: Sets up Kramer's confusion that will pay off later. Character-appropriate behavior.

jokes.text: Oh, God! It's a pig-man. A pig-man!
jokes.explanation: Iconic Kramer moment that launches a major subplot. Highly quotable and character-driven.

jokes.text: I pulled it in perfectly equidistant... from the car in front and behind. -Would you shut up, George?
jokes.explanation: Good callback to George's character trait, Elaine's reaction feels authentic.

jokes.text: -Me? A godfather? -Yes. Never go against the family, Elaine.
jokes.explanation: Obvious pop culture reference without much twist. Gets a mild laugh but lacks originality.

jokes.text: I'm talking about a pig-man. I walked in the wrong room, and there he was. -A pig-man? -A pig-man. Half pig, half man.
jokes.explanation: Establishes one of the episode's most memorable running gags. Kramer's matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Elaine, never ask me about my business.
jokes.explanation: Repetitive use of same pop culture reference without adding anything new. Feels lazy.

jokes.text: So was sacrificing virgins to appease the gods... but we don't do that anymore. -Well, maybe we should.
jokes.setup: Kramer compares circumcision to ancient traditions
jokes.punchline: George's casual agreement with virgin sacrifice
jokes.explanation: Dark humor escalation with perfect economy. George's casual response to something horrific is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: That's the guy I told where the elevator was.
jokes.setup: Man on roof about to jump
jokes.punchline: Kramer's helpful directions led to this
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that pays off earlier setup. Dark irony with good economy.

jokes.text: My car!
jokes.setup: Man jumps and lands on George's car
jokes.punchline: George's immediate concern for his car over human life
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing George's priorities. Maximum economy and character integration.

jokes.text: A mohel? What the hell is a mohel?... Motels, models... How do you find a mohel?
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay joke that works but isn't particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: What'd you think? It had no face, no personality. It was like a Martian.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking George about seeing circumcised penis
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor with an unexpected comparison. George's matter-of-fact delivery works well.

jokes.text: Swan dives from 20 floors up, lands right on top. What, do I got a bull's-eye up there? He couldn't move over two feet, huh? Land on the sidewalk, it's city property.
jokes.punchline: His complete lack of empathy, only concerned about property damage
jokes.explanation: Perfect George selfishness. The bull's-eye line and property distinction show his warped thinking brilliantly.

jokes.text: How about our little Elaine, huh? Attended the finest schools on the eastern seaboard... equestrian competitions, debutante balls. Look at her now. Interviewing mohels.
jokes.punchline: Contrast with her current mundane task
jokes.explanation: Good character work highlighting life's unexpected turns. Well-structured contrast.

jokes.text: Pig-man, baby, pig-man. If I have to hear about this pig-man one more time...
jokes.setup: Kramer connecting hospital DNA research to pig-man
jokes.explanation: Good running gag development and Jerry's straight-man reaction, though not particularly innovative.

jokes.text: They're probably creating a whole army of pig warriors.
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of absurdist concept. 'Pig warriors' is a perfect Kramer-esque leap of logic.

jokes.text: I'll tell you something. I wish there were pig-men. Get a few pig-men walking around, suddenly I'm looking a lot better. If someone wanted to fix me up, they could say, 'At least he's no pig-man.'
jokes.punchline: Wanting pig-men to exist to make him look better by comparison
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character insight showing George's warped logic and insecurity. Highly quotable and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Believe me, there'd be plenty of women going for these pig-men. Whatever the deformity, there's always some group attracted to it. 'That little tail really turns me on.'
jokes.setup: George theorizing about pig-men dating prospects
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of George's logic with specific absurd detail. The tail line is memorable.

jokes.text: Perhaps one day when the pig-men roam free, it will be stopped, Kramer. Until then, off with their heads.
jokes.punchline: Elaine's mock-serious response about pig-men liberation
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Elaine's sardonic wit. Combines multiple running gags effectively.

jokes.text: Besides, it makes sex more pleasurable. Yeah. So how does that help me?
jokes.explanation: Classic George self-pity with perfect economy. Character-appropriate response.

jokes.text: George, you ever see one? Yeah, my roommate in college. Yeah, what'd you think? I got used to it.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking about George's experience with uncircumcised penis
jokes.explanation: Simple callback to earlier discussion with typical George pragmatism. Functional but not memorable.

jokes.text: Believe me, Jerry, somewhere in this hospital... the anguished oink of pig-man cries out for help.
jokes.explanation: 'Anguished oink' is a brilliant phrase that's become quotable. Perfect Kramer dramatic flair.

jokes.text: Wait, George. You got room in the car for the pig-man, huh? The pig-man can take the bus.
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Kramer's empathy and George's selfishness. Well-executed callback.

jokes.text: George, if the pig-man had a car, he would give you a ride. How do you know? What if the pig-man had a two-seater?
jokes.punchline: George's practical objection about car size
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation where both characters stay true to type while discussing absurd scenario.

jokes.text: Horrible thing. Flew right past the children's wing. All the sick children in the playroom, looking out the window... just traumatized by the incident. Apparently they thought he was flying. You know how children are. 'Oh, look, a man is flying. A man is flying!' And then splat.
jokes.setup: George describing the suicide's impact on children
jokes.punchline: Children thinking man was flying, then 'splat'
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with perfect buildup and payoff. The children's perspective adds absurd tragedy.

jokes.text: That's where I come in. On 'splat.'
jokes.punchline: His focus on his own involvement at the worst moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect George selfishness with maximum economy. The timing and phrasing are excellent.

jokes.text: Now, of course, I can't help but feel that... had it been a convertible, this whole tragedy might have been averted. But I've never really been the kind of guy to buy a convertible... what with the baldness and everything.
jokes.setup: George trying to blame the car type
jokes.explanation: Classic George logic trying to blame everything but himself, with typical insecurity reveal.

jokes.text: I just can't help but think... that the hospital is somehow responsible for compensating... the other still-living victim of this horrendous, horrendous tragedy.
jokes.punchline: Calling himself a 'victim' of someone else's suicide
jokes.explanation: George's shameless self-victimization is perfectly in character. Good dramatic irony.

jokes.text: Well, profit... I think you'll see from the estimate that I'm not really profiting that much. It might be a little high, but...
jokes.setup: Administrator accuses George of profiting from tragedy
jokes.punchline: George's tone-deaf response about the estimate
jokes.explanation: George's complete social obliviousness on full display. Perfectly cringe-worthy.

jokes.text: Listen, you little quack. There was a half man, half pig in that room over there. Now, where is he? Where is he?! Half what? You know what I mean! Pork. Sausage. 'That's all, folks.'
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with Kramer becoming increasingly unhinged. The Porky Pig reference ties it together nicely.

jokes.text: I don't know why he asked me to be godfather. We're not close. Because we're on the softball team... I'm pitcher, he's catcher, he thinks we have a special relationship?
jokes.explanation: Good Jerry observational humor about relationship assumptions. Solid but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Don't they have friends? They're level-jumping on our friendship. Yes, it is level-jumping.
jokes.setup: Jerry's complaint about being asked to be godparent
jokes.explanation: 'Level-jumping' is a classic Seinfeld neologism that perfectly captures a relatable social frustration.

jokes.text: We're not talking about a manicure. Imagine, this is gonna be his first memory... of his parents just standing there while some stranger... cuts off a piece of his manhood and then serves a catered lunch.
jokes.setup: Jerry describing the bris from baby's perspective
jokes.punchline: The absurd juxtaposition with catered lunch
jokes.explanation: Excellent buildup to absurd conclusion. The catered lunch detail makes it memorably absurd.

jokes.text: What was that? Jeez, it scared the hell out of me! I mean, my God, I almost had a heart attack!
jokes.explanation: Sets up the mohel's nervous character. Functional character establishment.

jokes.text: Is the baby gonna cry like that? Is that how he cries... with that loud, sustained, squealing cry? That could pose a problem. Do have any control over your child?
jokes.punchline: Asking parents to control their baby
jokes.explanation: Good character development for nervous mohel. The control question is absurdly demanding.

jokes.text: I'll be lying in the gutter like a bum, a dog, a mutt, a mongrel, an animal!
jokes.explanation: Good escalation technique showing mohel's paranoid mindset. Well-executed but not exceptional.

jokes.text: See where that glass is? See how the glass is near the edge? You've got the whole table to put the glass. Why you chose the absolute edge so half the glass is hanging off the table. You breathe, and it falls over.
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work showing mohel's anxiety manifesting as control issues. Very relatable neurosis.

jokes.text: Two years later, you're barefoot... you step on broken glass and kill yourself! Is that what you want? I don't think that's what you want. Do you?
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from glass placement to death. Perfect neurotic mohel character development.

jokes.text: People, people. Compose yourself. This is a bris! We're performing a bris here, not a burlesque show. This is not a school play. This is not a baggy-pants farce. This is a bris!
jokes.punchline: Mohel's increasingly desperate attempts at dignity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing mohel losing control. The specific comparisons are well-chosen.

jokes.text: I could have been a kosher butcher like my brother. The money's good. It's got a union with benefits. And cows don't have families. You make a mistake with a cow, you move on with your life.
jokes.punchline: Cows don't have families - no emotional consequences
jokes.explanation: Brilliant insight about professional responsibility. The cow families line is perfectly quotable and dark.

jokes.text: That damn mohel! He circumcised my finger. -The mohel circumcised my finger. -You flinched.
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation of mohel's incompetence. Highly quotable and memorable physical comedy setup.

jokes.text: Another great spot in front of the hospital. In an emergency yet! How lucky are you, huh? Is that unbelievable? How unbelievable is that, huh?
jokes.punchline: George obsessing over parking spot during crisis
jokes.explanation: Perfect George character moment - completely missing the point during crisis. Excellent callback.

jokes.text: Well, if it isn't Shaky the Mohel. Nice circumcision, but it's not supposed to be a finger.
jokes.explanation: 'Shaky the Mohel' is a great nickname. Good callback with perfect Kramer delivery.

jokes.text: One more peep, and I'll slice you like a smoked sturgeon.
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with specific Jewish food reference. Mohel character showing his edge.

jokes.text: I'll get you for this. This is my business, my life! No one ruins it for me, no one!
jokes.explanation: Standard villain exit line but fits mohel's dramatic character. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: You really do the worst Godfather I've ever heard. You're not even close.
jokes.explanation: Simple callback to earlier running gag. Functional but adds little new.

jokes.text: It's a '76 Chevy Impala. They stole it right in front of the hospital. I saw the guy drive off in it. Well, he's about 5 feet tall... hairless... pink complexion. Looks like a pig.
jokes.explanation: Great callback that pays off the pig-man subplot. Good structure and timing.

jokes.text: And he's not a pig-man, is he? No, he's not. He's just a fat little mental patient.
jokes.explanation: Satisfying resolution to pig-man subplot. Good deflation of built-up mystery.

jokes.text: Don't ever go against the family, Jerry.
jokes.explanation: Overused callback that doesn't add anything new. Feels forced by this point.

jokes.text: To me, what's really amazing, that for every job that there is in the world... there's someone willing to do it. Someone goes, 'I will stand in the tunnel breathing exhaust... watching the cars go by, making sure everything's okay.'
jokes.punchline: Specific example of unpleasant tunnel job
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry observational humor. Good setup for the closing bit.

jokes.text: Someone goes, 'I will work behind the elephant with the big shovel.' Doctors go, 'Yes, I will confine myself... to one particularly objectionable part of the human body... all day, every day. I will do it.'
jokes.punchline: Doctors specializing in objectionable body parts
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from elephant handler to doctors. Ties back to episode themes nicely.

jokes.text: I think people that are unemployed are not really unable to find work... they're just easily disgusted. 'Yes, I'm starving, and my family has no clothing or shelter... but I'm not cleaning that up.'
jokes.explanation: Strong closing observation with good structure. Typical Jerry insight about human behavior.
